topic: In China employee termination: advanced cost-risk matrix for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination cost risk matrix foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Core framework for route-based risk and consequence mapping.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Supports matrix interpretation for practical route handling.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Adds dispute-facing evidence and consistency risk lens for matrix weighting.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors statutory closure controls in total-risk costing.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides multinational-practical context for matrix decision tradeoffs.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from scenario-led structure to cost-risk matrix structure while preserving legal source hierarchy.
Excluded low-fit tax-heavy and generalized comparative sources to keep matrix dimensions legal-governance centered.
Retained China Briefing as practical translation layer for foreign clients.
consensus: Cost-only optimization is often misleading in China termination decisions.
Risk-adjusted decisions require legal route confidence and evidence integrity weighting.
Matrix framing helps cross-border stakeholders align on defensibility, not just payout speed.
uncertainties: City-level implementation and evidentiary standards vary.
Complex payment-component treatment may require local confirmation.
Official English text may not reflect all Chinese-language practice nuances.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for cost-risk matrix format because sources support legal route grounding, dispute-risk weighting, and practical multinational interpretation.
